questions.text: What is the size of parvovirus?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: 200nm is too large for a parvovirus. Parvoviruses are actually among the smallest DNA viruses.
This option is incorrect. Parvoviruses are the smallest known animal viruses, measuring only 18–26 nanometers in diameter.
Parvoviruses are non-enveloped, icosahedral particles 18 to 26 nm in diameter. Plus and minus DNA strands are packaged into separate virions in approximately equal proportions.
This option is incorrect and is not the size of parvovirus. Parvoviruses are the smallest known animal viruses, measuring only 18–26 nanometers in diameter.

questions.explanation: Parvoviruses are the smallest known animal viruses, measuring only 18–26 nanometers in diameter. They are non-enveloped viruses with an icosahedral capsid and contain a single-stranded DNA (ssDNA) genome. Because of their tiny size and simple structure, parvoviruses rely heavily on the host cell’s machinery for replication. An example is the human parvovirus B19, which causes fifth disease in children.

questions.text: Eukaryotic, multicellular, and consumers belong to _ kingdom.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Kingdom Monera includes unicellular prokaryotes. Eukaryotic, multicellular organisms that act as consumers are placed in the Animalia kingdom.
Kingdom Protista includes organisms that are both unicellular as well as multicellular. Eukaryotic, multicellular organisms that act as consumers are placed in the Animalia kingdom.
Fungi are multicellular, eukaryotic but they are not consumers but rather have an absorptive mode of nutrition.

Eukaryotic, multicellular organisms that act as consumers are placed in the Animalia kingdom. These organisms don’t make their own food; instead, they rely on eating plants, other animals, or organic material to get energy. Their cells have a true nucleus, and their bodies are usually organized into tissues, organs, and organ systems. The Animalia kingdom includes everything from simple sponges all the way to complex animals like mammals.

questions.explanation: Kingdom Monera: It comprises unicellular, prokaryotic organisms, which can be heterotrophic or autotrophic, having no well-defined nucleus and no organelles.Kingdom Protista: Protists are eukaryotic, unicellular organisms They can be autotrophs or heterotrophs.Kingdom Fungi: Fungi can be single-celled or very complex multicellular organisms. All fungi are heterotrophic, which means that they get the energy they need to live from other organisms.They are eukaryotes.Kingdom Animalia: They are eukaryotic, multicellular, and heterotrophic.

questions.text: The numbers of capsomeres found in adenovirus capsid is?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Herpesviruses also have an icosahedral capsid, but theirs is made of 162 capsomeres. These capsomeres form a strong, symmetric shell around the viral DNA. Compared to adenoviruses, the herpes capsid is larger and sits within an additional layer called the tegument, followed by the outer envelope.

This option is not correct for an adenovirus. The whole particle of adenovirus is about 70 to 100 nanometers across. The shell itself is built from 252 repeating protein subunits called capsomeres, which fit together like pieces of a geometric puzzle to form the protective coat around the DNA.

The adenovirus particle consists of an icosahedral protein shell surrounding a protein core that contains the linear, double-stranded DNA genome. The shell, which is 70 to 100 nm in diameter, is made up of 252 structural capsomeres.

This option is not correct for an adenovirus. The whole particle of adenovirus is about 70 to 100 nanometers across. The shell itself is built from 252 repeating protein subunits called capsomeres, which fit together like pieces of a geometric puzzle to form the protective coat around the DNA.

questions.explanation: The adenovirus particle consists of an icosahedral protein shell surrounding a protein core that contains the linear, double-stranded DNA genome. The shell, which is 70 to 100 nm in diameter, is made up of 252 structural capsomeres. 162 capsomeres are found in herpes virus. So, option C is correct.

questions.text: Nowadays every newborn gets regular shots of vaccine for polio. It contains _ for polio to make a child immune against this disease.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Antibiotics are a class of medications used to treat bacterial infections. They work by either killing bacteria or inhibiting their growth and reproduction. Antibiotics are specifically designed to target bacteria and have no effect on viruses.

Antibodies, also known as immunoglobulins, are proteins produced by the immune system in response to the presence of foreign substances called antigens. Antibodies play a crucial role in the immune response and provide defense against harmful pathogens, such as bacteria, viruses, and other microorganisms.

The vaccine for polio contains antigens, specifically the inactivated or weakened forms of the poliovirus. These antigens stimulate the immune system to produce an immune response, including the production of antibodies, without causing the actual disease.

Antisera is a blood-derived product that contains antibodies specifically targeted against a particular antigen or group of antigens. It is obtained from the blood of animals or humans that have been immunized with the antigen of interest.

questions.explanation: A vaccine elicits an immune response by the host so that the host is better equipped to fight against the invading organism, in any potential future instance, via the production of memory cells. It may do so by containing the antigens of the invading organism (in this case, poliovirus) so that the host’s immune system produces memory cells against the specific organism, thereby conferring active immunity against the disease. Antibiotics (option A) are not present in vaccines; they are drugs used to treat bacterial illnesses. Antibodies (option B) are not present in vaccines; they are proteins, produced by plasma cells, to ward off infection. They are produced by the immune system to fight an active infection, but they are not used in vaccines since they do not confer active immunity as the host does not produce memory cells against the invading organism. Antisera (option D) is a blood serum preparation that contains antibodies against a certain antigen or disease; they are used as a treatment for certain conditions and do not confer active immunity to the individual.

questions.text: When were bacteriophages discovered by Twort?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Frederick W. Twort, a British bacteriologist, first discovered the bacteriophage phenomenon in 1915.He observed a “glassy transformation” in colonies of Staphylococcus and suggested it was caused by a filterable virus-like agent that could destroy bacteria.
The option is not correct. Bacteriophages were discovered by Frederick Twort in 1915, not 1920.
Frederick W. Twort, a British bacteriologist, first discovered the bacteriophage phenomenon in 1915.
Bacteriophages were discovered independently by Frederick W. Twort in Great Britain (1915) and Félix d'Hérelle in France (1917).

questions.explanation: Bacteriophages were first discovered by Frederick W. Twort in 1915. While working with Staphylococcus colonies, he noticed that some of them suddenly became transparent and failed to grow. Twort suggested that this was caused by a “filterable virus” capable of destroying bacteria, although he was uncertain and did not pursue the finding further.

questions.text: HIV is a:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: HIV is a virus. Viruses are small, infectious agents that can only replicate inside living cells. They are made up of genetic material (DNA or RNA) surrounded by a protein coat. HIV is a retrovirus, which means that its genetic material is RNA. HIV is able to infect and damage the cells of the immune system, which makes it difficult for the body to fight off infections.

Prions are infectious proteins that can cause diseases such as Creutzfeldt-Jakob disease. Prions are misfolded proteins that can cause other proteins to misfold. This can lead to the destruction of brain cells and the development of neurological symptoms.

Viroids are even smaller than viruses and do not have a protein coat. They are made up of only RNA and can cause diseases such as potato spindle tuber viroid. Viroids are able to infect plants and cause a variety of symptoms, including stunted growth, leaf curling, and wilting.

Venom is a poisonous substance that is produced by animals such as snakes, spiders, and scorpions. Venom is injected into the victim's body through the animal's fangs or stinger. Venom can cause a variety of symptoms, depending on the animal that produced it. Some venoms can be fatal, while others only cause mild discomfort.

questions.text: Which of the following diseases is sexually transmitted?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Tuberculosis is caused by bacteria that spread from person to person through microscopic droplets released into the air. This can happen when someone with the untreated, active form of tuberculosis coughs, speaks, sneezes, spits, laughs, or sings. Hence, Option A is incorrect.

Acquired immunodeficiency syndrome (AIDS) is a chronic, potentially life-threatening condition caused by the human immunodeficiency virus (HIV). By damaging your immune system, HIV interferes with your body's ability to fight infection and disease. You can get HIV from contact with infected blood, semen, or vaginal fluids. Most people get the virus by having unprotected sex with someone who has HIV. Another common way of getting it is by sharing drug needles with someone who is infected with HIV. Hence, Option B is correct.

When a mosquito bites a person infected with a dengue virus, the virus enters the mosquito. Then, when the infected mosquito bites another person, the virus enters that person's bloodstream and causes an infection. Hence, Option C is incorrect.

A person can get cholera by drinking water or eating food contaminated with cholera bacteria. In an epidemic, the source of the contamination is usually the feces of an infected person that contaminates water or food. Hence, Option D is incorrect.

questions.text: What type of virus is the smallpox virus?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: These viruses have a DNA genome surrounded by a capsid and an envelope. They often establish persistent infections.Examples: Herpes simplex virus (Herpesviridae), Hepatitis B virus (Hepadnaviridae), Poxvirus (Poxviridae), Cytomegalovirus (Herpesviridae).

These viruses have an RNA genome surrounded by both a capsid and a lipid envelope. The envelope helps them enter host cells but makes them more sensitive to drying and detergents.Examples: Influenza virus (Orthomyxoviridae), Human Immunodeficiency Virus (HIV, Retroviridae), Hepatitis C virus (Flaviviridae), Rabies virus (Rhabdoviridae).

These viruses contain DNA within a naked capsid, without an envelope. They are usually very resistant to environmental stress.Examples: Adenovirus (Adenoviridae), Human papillomavirus (Papillomaviridae), Parvovirus (Parvoviridae), and Polyomavirus (Polyomaviridae).

These viruses have an RNA genome enclosed only within a protein capsid (no envelope). This makes them more stable in harsh environments.Examples: Poliovirus (Picornaviridae), Rotavirus (Reoviridae), Norovirus (Caliciviridae), and Hepatitis A virus (Picornaviridae).

questions.explanation: Viruses are classified by genome type and presence of an envelope. RNA-enveloped viruses (e.g., influenza, HIV) have RNA with a capsid and lipid coat, while RNA-non-enveloped viruses (e.g., polio, rotavirus) carry RNA only in a capsid. DNA-enveloped viruses (e.g., herpes, hepatitis B, and poxvirus) contain a large double-stranded DNA genome with both capsid and envelope, whereas DNA-non-enveloped viruses (e.g., adenovirus and papillomavirus) package DNA only in a protein shell. Notably, the poxvirus is a DNA-enveloped virus.

questions.text: Which was the first virus to be successfully crystallized?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: TMV (tobacco mosaic virus) is the first virus to be successfully crystallized.The filterable agents were first purified in 1935, when Stanley was successful in crystallizing the tobacco mosaicvirus.

TMV was the first virus ever crystallized. In 1935, Stanley managed to purify these filterable infectious agents and obtained crystals of the tobacco mosaic virus, marking a major milestone in virology.

TMV became the first virus to be crystallized when Stanley, in 1935, purified these filterable agents and produced crystals of the tobacco mosaic virus. This discovery helped confirm that viruses could be studied like chemical substances.

In 1935, Stanley purified the tobacco mosaic virus and crystallized it, making TMV the first virus to be successfully crystallized. This work showed that viruses could exist in a form similar to simple chemical compounds.

questions.explanation: TMV (tobacco mosaic virus) is the first virus to be successfully crystallized.The filterable agents were first purified in 1935, when Stanley was successful in crystallizing the tobacco mosaic virus.

questions.text: Binomial nomenclature was proposed for the first time by:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: It is incorrect because Charles Darwin put forward the idea that species change over time through a process he called natural selection. This theory explains how organisms better suited to their environment are more likely to survive and pass on their traits.
It is incorrect because Rudolph Virchow dismissed the concept of abiogenesis, arguing instead that all living cells arise from pre-existing cells.
It is incorrect because Louis Pasteur made different contributions in the field of biology, such as the development of vaccines and pasteurisation of milk.
The first system of naming species on the basis of their classification was proposed by Carolus Linneaus called binomial nomenclature in 1707 hence it is correct.

questions.explanation: Tyrannosaurus rex is probably the most widely known binomial. The formal introduction of this system of naming species is credited to Carl Linnaeus, effectively beginning with his work Species Plantarum in 1753.

questions.text: Chicken pox is caused by:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Hepatitis A virus (HAV) primarily causes liver inflammation and is transmitted through contaminated food and water, not through direct contact or respiratory means associated with chickenpox.

The varicella zoster virus (VZV) is the causative agent of chickenpox, known for its characteristic itchy rash and blisters. It spreads easily through respiratory droplets and direct contact with the lesions.
Although the influenza virus leads to respiratory symptoms like fever and cough, it does not cause the distinct skin rash typical of chickenpox.
The human immunodeficiency virus (HIV) affects the immune system and is transmitted through bodily fluids. It does not cause chickenpox or its symptoms.

questions.explanation: Chickenpox (varicella) is an infectious disease caused by the vancella zoster virus (VZV), a member of the herpesvirus family. The virus leads to an itchy rash and fluid-filled blisters, typically in children. The same virus can reactivate later in life as shingles.

questions.text: The viruses are:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Viruses are not considered cellular organisms because they lack structures essential to cells, such as a cell membrane and organelles. They cannot independently carry out life processes like metabolism or reproduction.

Prokaryotes, such as bacteria, are unicellular organisms with a simpler structure than eukaryotes but still have cell membranes and cytoplasm. Viruses lack these features and are not classified as prokaryotes.

Viruses are non-cellular entities made up of genetic material encased in a protein coat. They do not possess cellular structures or the ability to perform cellular functions independently, making them distinct from all cellular life forms.
Eukaryotes have complex cells with a nucleus and organelles. Viruses lack these cellular components and cannot perform the functions of eukaryotic cells, such as energy production and reproduction.

Viruses are extremely small, typically ranging from 20 to 300 nanometers, which is far too small to be seen without a microscope. Observing viruses requires advanced techniques like electron microscopy.

questions.explanation: Viruses are classified as non-cellular because they lack the cellular structures that define living cells, such as a cell membrane, cytoplasm, and organelles. Instead, they consist of genetic material (either DNA or RNA) surrounded by a protein coat called a capsid. Unlike cellular organisms, viruses cannot perform metabolic processes or reproduce on their own; they must infect a host cell to replicate. This distinguishes them from cellular life forms, including prokaryotes and eukaryotes.Option A (Cellular) is incorrect because viruses do not have the structures that define cellular life. Option B (Prokaryotes) is incorrect because viruses do not fit the definition of prokaryotic cells, which have a cell membrane and cytoplasm. Option D (Eukaryotes) is incorrect because viruses do not have the complex structures found in eukaryotic cells. Option E (Visible with naked eye) is incorrect because viruses are too small to be seen without specialized equipment.

questions.text: Closely related species are grouped together into _ .
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: A family consists of multiple genera that share certain similarities. It is a broader classification than genus.
An order is a higher taxonomic category that encompasses multiple families. It is broader than both genus and family.
A phylum is a major taxonomic group that includes multiple classes. It is much broader than genus, family, and order.
A kingdom is one of the highest levels of taxonomic classification, comprising multiple phyla. It is far broader than genus.

Closely related species are grouped into a Genus. The genus is a key taxonomic rank just above species and below family. Organisms within a genus share genetic similarities and often resemble each other.

questions.explanation: Closely related species are grouped together into a “Genus”.

questions.text: Which of the following is the simplest form of pathogens causing diseases?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Viruses, while simple compared to many other life forms, still consist of genetic material (DNA or RNA) encapsulated in a protein coat. They require a host cell to reproduce and cannot independently cause disease, making them more complex than prions.

Prions are infectious agents that consist solely of misfolded proteins without any nucleic acid. They are considered the simplest form of pathogens because they lack cellular structure and replication machinery, which distinguishes them from other pathogens.

Fungi are complex organisms with defined cellular structures and reproductive mechanisms. They require specific environmental conditions to grow and are not the simplest pathogens, as they are multicellular or unicellular with extensive biological functions.

Amoebas are single-celled organisms that can cause diseases, but they are more complex than prions, as they possess cellular structures, organelles, and various pathogenic mechanisms. Thus, they do not represent the simplest form of pathogens.

questions.explanation: Prions are the simplest disease-causing pathogens because they are merely misfolded proteins with with no nucleic acid (no DNA or RNA), making them the most minimal infectious agents compared to viruses, fungi, or protozoa like amoeba.

questions.text: Mark the one Kingdom having the most conspicuous living organisms?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Monera includes single-celled organisms like bacteria and archaea, which are small and often not easily noticeable.
Protista includes single-celled organisms like algae and amoeba, which can be larger than bacteria but still not as easily noticeable as multicellular organisms.
Kingdom Animalia has the most conspicuous subsets of organisms because theoretically they have evolved the most and are the most advanced version of evolved species.
Plants are not conspicuous as animals because they don't move and their visibility is often limited.

questions.explanation: Kingdom Animalia has the most conspicous subsets of organims because theoretically they have evolved the most and are the most advanced version of evolved species.

questions.text: Which of the following is not a viral disease?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: AIDS is a viral illness that develops when a person is infected with the human immunodeficiency virus, or HIV. This virus weakens the immune system over time, making the body more vulnerable to infections and certain cancers.
Malaria is not a viral illness. It’s caused by protozoa of the genus Plasmodium, which are transmitted to humans through the bite of infected female Anopheles mosquitoes.
Influenza is a viral infection, and it’s caused by the influenza virus, which mainly affects the respiratory tract.
Chickenpox is a viral infection caused by the varicella-zoster virus. It typically leads to an itchy, blister-like rash along with fever and fatigue.

questions.explanation: Malaria is not a viral disease and is caused by the protozoan Plasmodium. All other options are viral infections that include AIDS caused by the HIV virus, influenza caused by the influenza virus, chickenpox caused by the varicella-zoster virus (VZV), and rabies caused by the rabies lyssavirus.

questions.text: Glucose forms a six cornered ring called:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Glucopyranose is the correct answer as it refers to the six-membered ring structure of glucose. This form is derived from the structure of pyran, a six-membered ring compound, and is the most stable form of glucose in solution.
Fructofuranose is incorrect because it is a five-membered ring structure of fructose, not glucose. It consists of four carbon atoms and one oxygen atom.
Ribofuranose is incorrect as it represents a five-membered ring structure of ribose, a different sugar. Ribose contains five carbon atoms.
Deoxyribofuranose is incorrect because it is a form of deoxyribose, a component of DNA, and forms a five-membered ring. It is neither glucose nor does it form a six-membered ring.

questions.explanation: Glucose forms a six-membered ring known as glucopyranose when it cyclizes. This structure is named after pyran, a six-membered ring compound, making it the most prevalent and stable form of glucose in aqueous solution. The other options—fructofuranose, ribofuranose, and deoxyribofuranose—are all furanose forms, implying they have five-membered rings. These structures correspond to different sugars or modified sugars, not glucose.

questions.text: In free state, glucose is present in:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: While glucose is abundant in grapes, it is not exclusive to this fruit alone.
Figs contain glucose, but like grapes, they are not the only fruit where glucose is found in free state.
Dates are rich in glucose, but glucose is not unique to dates alone.
This option is correct because glucose is present in a free state in all fruits, not just specific ones like grapes, figs, or dates.

questions.explanation: Glucose is a simple sugar commonly found in its free state in a variety of fruits. It is especially abundant in grapes, figs, and dates, but it is not limited to these fruits alone. Thus, the correct answer is that glucose is present in all fruits in free state, making 'All fruits' the correct choice. Other options are incorrect because they suggest exclusivity to specific fruits.

questions.text: These are comparatively less sweet in taste and less soluble in water:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Monosaccharides are simple sugars that are highly soluble in water and generally sweet in taste. They consist of single sugar molecules and are fundamental units of carbohydrates.

Oligosaccharides consist of 3 to 10 monosaccharide units and are known for being less sweet and less soluble in water compared to monosaccharides and disaccharides. They have more complex structures, which reduces their sweetness and solubility.
Disaccharides are composed of two monosaccharide molecules. They are generally sweet and soluble in water, similar to monosaccharides, making them unsuitable as the correct answer.

Polysaccharides are complex carbohydrates composed of many monosaccharide units. They are not sweet and are less soluble in water than other carbohydrate types, but they are more complex than oligosaccharides.

questions.explanation: Oligosaccharides are the correct answer because they consist of multiple monosaccharide units, typically ranging from 3 to 10. This complexity leads to a reduction in the sweetness and solubility in water compared to simpler carbohydrates like monosaccharides and disaccharides. Monosaccharides and disaccharides are generally sweet and highly soluble in water, whereas polysaccharides, although also less sweet and soluble, are much more complex than oligosaccharides.

questions.text: The molecular formula of sucrose is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This is the molecular formula of sucrose, a disaccharide composed of glucose and fructose units. The formula C12H22O11 accurately represents the arrangement of carbon, hydrogen, and oxygen atoms in sucrose.
This formula is for glucose, a monosaccharide. Glucose is a component of sucrose but does not represent sucrose itself, which is a disaccharide.
This formula corresponds to glycerol, a triol, not a sugar. Therefore, it is not the formula for sucrose.
This formula does not correspond to any common carbohydrate or known compound relevant to sucrose. It is incorrect for sucrose.

questions.explanation: Sucrose is a disaccharide formed by the condensation of glucose and fructose monomers, resulting in the removal of a water molecule (H2O). This process forms a 1,2-glycosidic bond, leading to the molecular formula C12H22O11, making Option A correct. Options B, C, and D represent different compounds: glucose, glycerol, and an undefined carbohydrate structure, respectively. They do not correspond to the molecular composition of sucrose.

questions.text: Starches are of _ types.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This answer is incorrect. Starches are not composed of just one type. They consist of two main types: amylose and amylopectin. Amylose has a linear structure, while amylopectin is branched, both contributing to the characteristics of starch.

This answer is correct. Starches are made up of two main types: amylose and amylopectin. Amylose features a linear chain of glucose units, and amylopectin has a highly branched structure, which together define the properties of starch.
This answer is incorrect. Starches do not have three types. The main types of starch are amylose and amylopectin, which are only two in number.
This answer is incorrect. Starches are not divided into four types. Their primary classification includes only amylose and amylopectin, which are the two main components.

questions.explanation: Starches consist of two major types: amylose and amylopectin. Amylose is characterized by a linear chain of glucose molecules, while amylopectin is distinguished by its highly branched structure. These two components play a critical role in determining the properties and functionality of starch. The options suggesting one, three, or four types are incorrect because they do not align with the scientific classification of starch.

questions.text: It is found abundantly in muscle and liver cells:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Starch is a carbohydrate that acts as a storage form of glucose in plants, not in animals. It is abundant in plant cells but not in muscle and liver cells, which are characteristic of animal glycogen storage.

Chitin is a structural polysaccharide found in the exoskeletons of arthropods and the cell walls of fungi. It is not involved in energy storage in muscle or liver cells, making it irrelevant for this context.

Glycogen is the primary storage form of glucose in animals. It is stored abundantly in muscle and liver cells, providing a quick source of energy when needed. This makes it the correct choice for this question.

Amylose is a component of starch, a plant storage polysaccharide. While it shares a structural similarity with glycogen, it is not found in animal muscle or liver cells, making it incorrect in this context.

questions.explanation: Glycogen is the correct answer because it is the main storage form of glucose in animals, found abundantly in muscle and liver cells. This polysaccharide is used as a readily available energy source. In contrast, starch and amylose are plant-based storage forms of glucose, and chitin is a structural component in certain organisms, not related to energy storage in animals.

questions.text: In the herbivores, cellulose is digested because of:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Bacteria are crucial for cellulose digestion in herbivores. They produce cellulase enzymes that break down cellulose, allowing herbivores to absorb the resulting sugars. They are primarily found in the rumen or cecum of herbivores such as cows and sheep.
Yeasts are not significant contributors to cellulose digestion in herbivores. They are more involved in fermentation processes and do not produce cellulases necessary for cellulose breakdown.

Protozoa, especially ciliates, contribute to cellulose digestion in herbivores by producing enzymes that break down cellulose. They exist in a symbiotic relationship with herbivores, residing in the rumen and aiding in the digestion process.

This option correctly identifies that bacteria and protozoa play significant roles in cellulose digestion in herbivores. Yeasts have a minimal impact, but the combination of these microorganisms facilitates effective cellulose breakdown in herbivores' digestive systems.

questions.explanation: In herbivores, the digestion of cellulose is primarily facilitated by bacteria and protozoa present in their digestive systems. These microorganisms produce enzymes that break down cellulose into simpler sugars, which the herbivores can then absorb. Bacteria and protozoa are critical for this process, residing in the rumen or cecum of animals like cows and sheep. Although yeasts are not primarily involved in cellulose digestion, they might contribute minimally. Therefore, the correct answer is Option D: Bacteria, Yeasts, and Protozoa. Options A and C are partially correct because they include either bacteria or protozoa, but not both. Option B, involving yeasts, is incorrect as yeasts are not major contributors to cellulose digestion.

questions.text: Some _ provide insulation against atmospheric heat and cold.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Lipids, particularly in the form of fats and oils, create an insulating layer that helps organisms maintain a stable internal temperature by reducing heat exchange with the environment. This is due to their low thermal conductivity.
Carbohydrates are mainly involved in energy storage and supply, as well as structural functions. They do not significantly contribute to insulation against temperature changes.
Nucleic acids such as DNA and RNA are central to genetic information storage and transmission. They do not play a role in thermal insulation.
Proteins serve diverse functions including catalysis, structural support, and transportation. They are not primarily responsible for providing thermal insulation.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A: Lipids. Lipids provide thermal insulation by forming a barrier that reduces heat exchange, allowing organisms to maintain a stable internal temperature. Fats and oils, which are types of lipids, are particularly effective in this role due to their low thermal conductivity. On the other hand, carbohydrates are primarily energy sources, nucleic acids are involved in genetic functions, and proteins have various roles but are not specialized for thermal insulation.

questions.text: A compound produced as a result of a chemical reaction of an alcohol with an acid, where a water molecule is released, is called:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Acylglycerols, also known as glycerides or triglycerides, are a type of lipid that consists of glycerol esterified with fatty acids. While they are esters formed from glycerol and fatty acids, they are not specifically related to the given definition in the question. Acylglycerols serve as a major storage form of fats in the body.

An ester is a compound produced from the reaction between an alcohol and an acid, resulting in the release of a water molecule. Esters have a general structure of RCOOR', where R and R' represent organic groups. They are commonly used in various applications, such as flavorings, fragrances, and solvents.

Fatty acids are carboxylic acids with long hydrocarbon chains. While they are involved in the formation of esters, fatty acids themselves do not match the given definition. Fatty acids are building blocks for various lipids, including triglycerides, phospholipids, and waxes.

Waxes are esters formed by the reaction of long-chain fatty acids with long-chain alcohols. While waxes are indeed formed through an esterification reaction, they are not the specific compound described in the question. Waxes serve as protective coatings in many organisms and objects.

questions.explanation: Correct option: B) The compound produced as a result of a chemical reaction of an alcohol with an acid, where a water molecule is released, is called an ester.

questions.text: Fatty acids contain even number of carbon atoms in straight chain attached with _ atoms and having _.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Fatty acids contain hydrogen atoms attached to an even-numbered carbon chain and have a carboxyl group (-COOH) at one end, which is responsible for their acidic properties.
Fatty acids do not have an alcohol group (-OH) attached to the carbon chain. They specifically have a carboxyl group, not an alcohol group.
While fatty acids do have oxygen in the carboxyl group, it is not correct to refer to the group as an 'acid group.' The correct term is 'carboxyl group,' which imparts acidic properties.
Fatty acids do not contain an alcohol group. The hydrogens are attached to the carbon atoms in the chain, and the carboxyl group is responsible for the acidic properties.

questions.explanation: Fatty acids are characterized by a long chain of carbon atoms that are typically even-numbered and saturated with hydrogen atoms. At one end of this chain, there is a carboxyl group (-COOH), which confers acidic properties to the molecule. This explains why Option A is correct. Option B is incorrect because fatty acids do not have an alcohol group, and Options C and D misrepresent the functional groups present in fatty acids. Option C incorrectly labels the carboxyl group as an 'acid group,' and Option D incorrectly associates an alcohol group with fatty acids.

questions.text: _ is much more soluble in organic solvent than _.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Acetic acid and butyric acid are both short-chain fatty acids, with acetic acid being more polar and hydrophilic due to its shorter chain. Therefore, acetic acid is not necessarily more soluble in organic solvents compared to butyric acid.
Butyric acid is a short-chain fatty acid and is more polar than palmitic acid. Therefore, it is less soluble in non-polar organic solvents compared to palmitic acid, a long-chain fatty acid.
Palmitic acid, a long-chain fatty acid, is more non-polar and hydrophobic, increasing its solubility in non-polar organic solvents compared to butyric acid, which is more polar and hydrophilic.
While both are long-chain fatty acids, oleic acid is unsaturated with a double bond, which typically increases solubility in organic solvents compared to saturated fatty acids like palmitic acid.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C: Palmitic acid is much more soluble in organic solvents than butyric acid. This is because palmitic acid, being a long-chain fatty acid with 16 carbon atoms, is more non-polar and hydrophobic, which aligns well with the non-polar nature of organic solvents. In contrast, butyric acid is a short-chain fatty acid with 4 carbon atoms, making it more polar and less compatible with non-polar organic solvents.Option A is incorrect because both acetic and butyric acids are short-chain fatty acids with significant polarity, making them less soluble in non-polar organic solvents.Option B is incorrect because butyric acid's shorter chain and higher polarity make it less soluble than palmitic acid in non-polar solvents.Option D is incorrect because oleic acid's unsaturation typically enhances solubility in organic solvents compared to saturated fatty acids like palmitic acid.

questions.text: Melting point of butyric acid is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option suggests a higher melting point commonly associated with longer-chain fatty acids. Butyric acid, being a short-chain fatty acid, has a significantly lower melting point.
This option is incorrect as it indicates a melting point typical of longer-chain fatty acids, not applicable to short-chain ones like butyric acid.
This option is incorrect. Such a high melting point is characteristic of even longer-chain or more complex fatty acids, not a simple, short-chain fatty acid like butyric acid.
The melting point of butyric acid is approximately -8°C, which is correct. This is due to its short carbon chain and simple structure.

questions.explanation: Butyric acid is a short-chain fatty acid with four carbon atoms, which results in a relatively low melting point of approximately -8°C. The other options represent melting points typical of longer-chain, more complex fatty acids. Therefore, these values are too high to be applicable to butyric acid.

questions.text: A saturated fatty acid with four carbon atoms is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Oleic acid (C18H34O2) is an unsaturated fatty acid with 18 carbon atoms, not 4.
Palmitic acid (C16H32O2) is a saturated fatty acid, but it has 16 carbon atoms, not 4.
Butyric acid (C4H8O2) is a saturated fatty acid with exactly 4 carbon atoms.
Acetic acid (C2H4O2) is a saturated fatty acid but contains only 2 carbon atoms, not 4.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Butyric acid, which is a saturated fatty acid with 4 carbon atoms, as indicated by the 'but-' prefix in its name. Other options have either more carbon atoms or are unsaturated. Oleic acid is unsaturated and has 18 carbon atoms. Palmitic acid is saturated but has 16 carbon atoms. Acetic acid, while saturated, has only 2 carbon atoms.

questions.text: Fats and oils are:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Fats and oils are lighter than water, as they have a lower density. So, this option is correct.
Fats and oils have a lower density, hence they are lighter than water. So, this option is not correct.
Fats and oils are more viscous than water. Viscosity refers to the resistance of a fluid to flow. So, this option is not correct.
Fats and oils are less dense than water. So, this option is not correct.

questions.explanation: Fats and oils have a specific gravity of about 0.8. That is why they are lighter and have a lower density than water.

questions.text: Glycerol, fatty acids and phosphoric acid gives rise to:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Phospholipids are a class of lipids that form a major component of cell membranes. They are derived from phosphatidic acid, but the additional bonding with a nitrogenous base is required to form a phospholipid. Thus, the direct combination of glycerol, fatty acids, and phosphoric acid forms phosphatidic acid, not a complete phospholipid.
Glycerol, fatty acids, and phosphoric acid combine to form phosphatidic acid. This molecule acts as a precursor for the biosynthesis of many other lipids.

Phosphatidylcholine is a specific type of phospholipid derived from phosphatidic acid. It requires the addition of choline as a nitrogenous base. Therefore, it is not directly formed by the combination of glycerol, fatty acids, and phosphoric acid alone.

Phosphatidylethanolamine is another type of phospholipid derived from phosphatidic acid, requiring ethanolamine as a nitrogenous base. Like phosphatidylcholine, it is not directly formed by the combination of glycerol, fatty acids, and phosphoric acid.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is phosphatidic acid. When glycerol, fatty acids, and phosphoric acid combine, they form phosphatidic acid, which is the simplest phospholipid and serves as a precursor for more complex lipids such as phospholipids. Phospholipids like phosphatidylcholine and phosphatidylethanolamine require additional components, such as a nitrogenous base (choline or ethanolamine, respectively), which are not part of the initial combination described in the question.

questions.text: The function of an enzyme is to:
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Cause chemical reactions that would not otherwise take place
options.explanation: Enzymes do not initiate new reactions; instead, they speed up reactions that would occur naturally, albeit slowly. Therefore, this option is incorrect.

options.text: Change the rate of a chemical reaction
options.explanation: Enzymes are catalysts that increase the rate of chemical reactions by lowering the activation energy, making this the correct option.

options.text: Change the direction of a chemical reaction
options.explanation: Enzymes do not alter the direction of reactions; they only increase the speed at which equilibrium is reached. Thus, this option is incorrect.

options.text: Raise the activation energy of a chemical reaction
options.explanation: Enzymes work by lowering the activation energy required for a reaction, not raising it. This makes the option incorrect.
Since option B correctly describes the function of enzymes, this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: Option A: Enzymes can not initiate a chemical reaction.They can only accelerate a chemical reaction.So,option A is incorrect.Option B: Enzymes accelerate a chemical reaction by reducing the activation energy of a specific chemical reaction.So,Option B is correct.Option C: Enzymes can not change the direction of a given reaction.They can only speed up an already started reaction.So,Option C is incorrect.Option D: Enzymes do not raise the activation energy.Enzymes actually decrease the activation energy to lower the energy required for the process.So, Option D is incorrect.Option E: We have already found the correct answer, so this option is incorrect.

questions.text: Most enzymes have an optimum temperature of around:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: 30°C is generally too low for the optimum functioning of enzymes in the human body, which typically thrive closer to body temperature.
40°C is close to the normal body temperature of humans, which is around 37°C. This temperature range is often optimal for enzymes in the human body, enabling them to function efficiently.
50°C is above the typical optimum temperature for human enzymes. At this temperature, some enzymes may start to denature, losing their functional shape.
20°C is significantly below the typical optimum temperature for enzymes in the human body, which generally function best around normal body temperature.

questions.explanation: Most enzymes have an optimum temperature at which they function most efficiently. For many enzymes in the human body, this optimum temperature is around 37 degrees Celsius (98.6 degrees Fahrenheit), which is the normal body temperature. However, the specific optimum temperature can vary for different enzymes. Hence here we will select 40 degrees Celsius.

questions.text: When an inhibitor binds to an enzyme other than the active site and alters its structure, then it is called:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Competitive inhibitors are a type of enzyme inhibitor that competes with the substrate for binding to the active site of an enzyme. These inhibitors resemble the structure of the substrate and can bind to the enzyme, but they do not undergo the same chemical reactions as the substrate.

A non-competitive inhibitor is a type of enzyme inhibitor that binds to an enzyme at a location other than the active site. This binding causes a conformational change in the enzyme, reducing its catalytic activity. Non-competitive inhibitors bind to a site on the enzyme that is distinct from the active site. This binding can occur whether or not the substrate is already bound to the enzyme. An example of a non-competitive inhibitor is heavy metal ions that can bind to enzymes, causing a decrease in catalytic activity.
A reversible inhibitor form weak linkages with the enzyme. Their efect can be neutralized completely or partly by an increase in the concentration of the substrate.

An irreversible inhibitor check the reaction rate by occupying the active sites or destroying the globular structure. They occupy the active sites by forming covalent bonds or they may physically block the active sites.

questions.explanation: A non-competitive inhibitor is a type of enzyme inhibitor that binds to an enzyme at a location other than the active site. This binding causes a conformational change in the enzyme, reducing its catalytic activity. Non-competitive inhibitors bind to a site on the enzyme that is distinct from the active site. This binding can occur whether or not the substrate is already bound to the enzyme. An example of a non-competitive inhibitor is heavy metal ions that can bind to enzymes, causing a decrease in catalytic activity.

questions.text: Holoenzyme is?

options.explanation: This refers to the apoenzyme, which is the protein part of an enzyme, but not the complete active form. Holoenzyme includes both the protein and non-protein parts needed for activity.
This refers to cofactors or coenzymes, which are non-protein components that assist enzyme activity. However, a holoenzyme includes both the protein and non-protein parts.
A holoenzyme is a complete, functional enzyme consisting of an apoenzyme (the protein part) and its necessary cofactors or coenzymes.
This refers to an apoenzyme, which is the inactive protein part of an enzyme. A holoenzyme is the active form.

questions.explanation: A holoenzyme is the complete, active form of an enzyme. It is composed of an apoenzyme, which is the protein portion, and one or more cofactors, which can be metal ions or organic molecules known as coenzymes. These components together allow the holoenzyme to perform its catalytic function. Option A and B are incorrect because they describe only parts of the enzyme system, not the complete active form. Option D is incorrect because it describes an inactive enzyme, which is an apoenzyme, not a holoenzyme.

questions.text: A non­-proteinaceous enzyme is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Lysozyme is a protein-based enzyme that functions as an antimicrobial agent by breaking down bacterial cell walls. It is not non-proteinaceous.
Ribozymes are RNA molecules that catalyze specific biochemical reactions, similar to protein enzymes, but they are not composed of proteins, making them non-proteinaceous enzymes.
Ligase is a protein enzyme that facilitates the joining of DNA strands during replication and repair processes. It is proteinaceous.
Deoxyribonuclease (DNase) is a protein enzyme that cleaves DNA molecules. It is not a non-proteinaceous enzyme.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Ribozyme. Ribozymes are unique because they are RNA molecules with catalytic properties, unlike typical enzymes, which are proteins. Ribozymes can catalyze reactions such as RNA cleavage and ligation, and they play crucial roles in the function of ribosomes. The other options, Lysozyme, Ligase, and Deoxyribonuclease, are all protein-based enzymes; hence, they do not fit the description of a non-proteinaceous enzyme.

questions.text: The role of an enzyme in reactions is to:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Enzymes are proteins that act as biological catalysts, facilitating chemical reactions in living organisms. One of their key functions is to decrease the activation energy required for a reaction to occur. Activation energy is the energy barrier that needs to be overcome for a chemical reaction to proceed. By lowering the activation energy, enzymes accelerate the rate of reactions without being consumed or permanently altered in the process. Enzymes achieve this by binding to specific molecules, called substrates, and bringing them into close proximity, thus facilitating the formation of new chemical bonds or the breaking of existing ones. This binding process occurs at the enzyme's active site, a specific region on the enzyme where the substrate binds and the reaction takes place. By providing an environment that promotes the reaction and stabilizes transition states, enzymes reduce the amount of energy required to initiate the reaction, ultimately lowering the activation energy.
Option "increase activation energy" is incorrect because enzymes actually work to lower the activation energy, not increase it.

Option "inorganic catalyst" is incorrect because enzymes are organic catalysts, composed of proteins, while inorganic catalysts are typically non-biological substances, such as metals or metal ions, that can also accelerate chemical reactions, called an activator.
This option is incorrect because option A is correct.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is: decrease activation energy. Enzymes are proteins that act as biological catalysts, facilitating chemical reactions in living organisms. One of their key functions is to decrease the activation energy required for a reaction to occur. Activation energy is the energy barrier that needs to be overcome for a chemical reaction to proceed. By lowering the activation energy, enzymes accelerate the rate of reactions without being consumed or permanently altered in the process. Enzymes achieve this by binding to specific molecules, called substrates, and bringing them into close proximity, thus facilitating the formation of new chemical bonds or the breaking of existing ones. This binding process occurs at the enzyme's active site, a specific region on the enzyme where the substrate binds and the reaction takes place. By providing an environment that promotes the reaction and stabilizes transition states, enzymes reduce the amount of energy required to initiate the reaction, ultimately lowering the activation energy.

questions.text: If the product of a pathway down-regulates its own synthesis, this phenomenon would be called:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Feedback inhibition is a regulatory mechanism where the end product of a metabolic pathway inhibits an enzyme involved in its own synthesis, thereby reducing its production. This helps maintain homeostasis and prevents the overaccumulation of the product.

Feedback regulation encompasses all feedback mechanisms in biological systems, including both positive and negative feedback. While it includes the concept of down-regulation, it is a broader term and does not specifically denote the inhibition of synthesis by the product itself.

Positive feedback refers to processes that enhance or amplify production. This is contrary to down-regulation and is therefore not applicable in this context, as it would lead to increased synthesis rather than a decrease.

Enzyme activation is the process of turning on an enzyme to increase its activity, which would promote the production of a product rather than inhibit it. This makes it an inappropriate choice for the question regarding down-regulation.

questions.explanation: Feedback inhibition is a specific type of regulation where the end product of a metabolic pathway acts to decrease its own production. This is done by inhibiting the activity of enzymes involved in its synthesis, helping to prevent excess accumulation of the product.

questions.text: Enzymes function most effectively over a narrow range of pH known as:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The optimum pH refers to the specific pH level at which an enzyme exhibits peak activity. Enzymes have a designated pH range that allows them to function most efficiently, making optimum pH a critical concept in enzymology.

This term describes the temperature at which an enzyme performs with maximum efficiency. While temperature is important for enzyme activity, it is not the same as pH, which is the focus of this question.
This refers to the amount of enzyme present in a reaction mixture. While enzyme concentration can influence reaction rates, it is unrelated to the pH levels at which enzymes are most effective.

This pertains to the amount of substrate that an enzyme acts upon. Although substrate concentration can affect enzyme activity, it does not relate to the pH level required for optimal enzyme function.

questions.explanation: Enzymes exhibit their highest activity at a specific pH level, referred to as the optimum pH. This is the range in which enzymes can catalyze reactions most effectively. The other options do not pertain to pH: optimum temperature relates to temperature conditions, enzyme concentration refers to the amount of enzyme, and substrate concentration relates to the availability of substrate. Therefore, understanding that optimum pH is crucial for enzyme function helps in identifying the correct answer.

questions.text: Coenzymes are closely related to:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Coenzymes are organic molecules that assist enzymes in catalyzing reactions, while hormones are regulatory molecules that send signals within the body. These two classes of biomolecules have different roles and are not directly related.

Inhibitors are substances that decrease enzyme activity, contrasting with coenzymes, which are necessary for enhancing enzyme function. Thus, they serve distinct purposes in biochemical reactions and are not related.
Antibodies are proteins produced by the immune system to identify and neutralize pathogens. They do not play a role in enzymatic processes like coenzymes do, making them unrelated.

Many coenzymes are derived from vitamins, which are essential organic compounds needed in small amounts for various metabolic functions. For instance, NAD (nicotinamide adenine dinucleotide), an important coenzyme, is derived from the vitamin niacin. This close relationship underscores the connection between coenzymes and vitamins.

questions.explanation: Coenzymes are vital organic molecules that assist enzymes in catalyzing biochemical reactions. They are often derived from vitamins, which are essential nutrients that organisms require for various physiological functions. For example, NAD is a well-known coenzyme that originates from niacin (vitamin B3). In contrast, hormones act as signaling molecules that regulate physiological processes, inhibitors serve to decrease enzyme activity, and antibodies function in the immune response, none of which are related to the role of coenzymes in enzymatic activity.

questions.text: Prosthetic groups are:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: While some prosthetic groups can include metallic ions, they are not exclusively metallic ions.
Prosthetic groups are primarily organic molecules that are tightly bound to enzymes, facilitating their function.
Prosthetic groups are not typically classified solely as inorganic molecules; they can include organic components.
Prosthetic groups are not radicals; they are stable molecules or ions, usually organic, that are tightly associated with proteins.

questions.explanation: Cofactors are substances that help enzymes function properly. Inorganic cofactors act as activators, while organic cofactors can be either coenzymes or prosthetic groups. Generally, prosthetic groups are organic molecules that remain tightly bound to the enzyme. However, they may sometimes be inorganic in nature, such as metallic ions. It is important to note that prosthetic groups are not free radicals.

questions.text: Allosteric enzymes have _ major site(s).
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: An allosteric enzyme requires more than one site to function, as it has both an active site and an allosteric site.
Allosteric enzymes have two major sites: an active site for substrate attachment and an allosteric site for binding regulatory molecules.
Three sites are not typical for allosteric enzymes; they primarily have an active site and an allosteric site.
Four sites are too many for standard allosteric enzymes; they usually have two key sites.

questions.explanation: Allosteric enzymes have, in addition to their active site for substrate binding, another site called the allosteric site. At this site, a molecule other than the substrate can bind. This binding regulates the enzyme’s activity by either enhancing or inhibiting its action.

questions.text: “Zymase”- a group of 14 enzymes used in the fermentation of starch, is present in:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: While the enzyme complex historically referred to as zymase is famously found in yeast, bacteria also possess the necessary enzymes to carry out alcoholic fermentation and other forms of glycolysis.
Zymase is an enzyme complex that catalyzes the fermentation of sugar into ethanol and carbon dioxide. It occurs naturally in yeasts. Zymase activity varies among yeast strains.
Option C is incorrect, as although zymase is present in yeast, which belongs to fungi, we cannot say that all fungi contain zymase.
Option D is incorrect as algae also do not contain zymase enzymes. Algae are photosynthetic organisms that produce carbohydrates (starches and sugars) but do not ferment them using zymase.
Option E is incorrect as viruses have entirely different sets of enzymes that are involved in transcription and cell division. Zymase enzymes are not present in viruses.

questions.explanation: Zymase is an enzyme complex that catalyzes the fermentation of sugar into ethanol and carbon dioxide. It occurs naturally in yeasts. Zymase activity varies among yeast strains. Zymase is also the brand name of the drug pancrelipase.

questions.text: A powerful digesting enzyme that is capable of destroying a cell's internal structure is called:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Lipase is an enzyme that primarily breaks down lipids (fats) into fatty acids and glycerol. While important for fat digestion, lipases do not destroy the internal structures of cells; they specifically target lipid molecules.

DNAase is an enzyme that degrades DNA molecules, which is crucial for processes involving genetic material. However, DNAases do not affect the overall internal structure of cells; they are specific to DNA degradation.

Phospholipase is an enzyme that breaks down phospholipids, which are key components of cell membranes. While it can disrupt cell membranes, phospholipases do not typically destroy the entire internal structure of a cell.

Pepsin is a powerful protein-digesting enzyme found in the stomach. It breaks down proteins into smaller peptides, which can lead to the degradation of the cell's internal structures, as proteins are essential components of cellular architecture. Therefore, pepsin is the correct answer.

questions.explanation: Pepsin is the correct answer because it is a potent protein-digesting enzyme that can effectively break down the proteins that make up much of a cell's internal structure. The other options, while they play important roles in digestion, do not target the overall internal cellular architecture. Lipase focuses on fats, DNAase targets DNA specifically, and phospholipase acts on membrane components but does not destroy internal cellular structures as pepsin does.

questions.text: Three of the following statements about enzymes are correct and one is wrong. Which one is wrong?
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Enzymes require optimum pH for maximal activity
options.explanation: This statement is correct. Enzymes have a specific pH range where they exhibit peak activity. A deviation from this optimal pH results in decreased activity due to alterations in enzyme structure or ionization of active sites.

options.text: Enzymes are denatured at high temperatures but in certain exceptional organisms, they are effective even at temperatures 80° – 90°C
options.explanation: This statement is correct. While most enzymes lose their structure and function at high temperatures, thermophilic organisms possess enzymes that remain stable and functional at temperatures between 80° – 90°C.

This statement is correct. Enzymes exhibit high specificity, typically catalyzing a single type of chemical reaction or acting on a specific substrate, due to the precise interaction between the enzyme and its substrate.

options.text: Most enzymes are proteins, but some are lipids
options.explanation: This statement is incorrect. Enzymes are primarily proteins, with some being catalytic RNA molecules known as ribozymes. There are no known enzymes that are composed of lipids, making this statement incorrect.

questions.explanation: This statement is incorrect. Enzymes are primarily proteins. While there are some RNA molecules with catalytic activity known as ribozymes, enzymes composed of lipids are not common. The majority of enzymes are indeed proteins. Therefore, the wrong statement is D. Enzymes are predominantly proteins, and the presence of lipid-based enzymes is not a common characteristic.

questions.text: The lower the activation energy, the _ the reaction will be.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This is the correct answer. Lowering the activation energy allows more reactant molecules to attain the necessary energy to reach the transition state, resulting in a significantly increased reaction rate.
This option is incorrect because a decrease in activation energy does not lead to a moderate reaction rate; rather, it accelerates the reaction speed, making it more rapid.
This option is incorrect because reducing the activation energy leads to a faster reaction rate, not a slower one, as it allows more molecules to undergo the reaction more quickly.

questions.explanation: Enzymes function as biological catalysts that significantly enhance the rate of chemical reactions by decreasing the activation energy required for the reaction to proceed. When activation energy is lowered, a larger proportion of the reactant molecules can successfully collide with sufficient. energy to undergo the reaction, thus leading to a faster reaction rate. The incorrect options fail to recognize that a decrease in activation energy exclusively benefits the speed of the reaction rather than maintaining a moderate or slower rate. In summary, the correct answer is faster because enzymes facilitate quicker reactions by reducing the energy barrier.

questions.text: _ suggested that each enzyme had a particular shape into which the substrate fit exactly.

questions.text: If the concentration of the substrate molecule is higher than the enzyme, then the rate of reaction would be:

options.explanation: When substrate concentration is higher than enzyme concentration, the reaction rate does not increase because the enzymes become saturated. They are already working at full capacity, so adding more substrate does not increase the rate.

When substrate concentration is higher than enzyme concentration, the enzymes are saturated, and the reaction rate remains constant. The enzymes are operating at their maximum efficiency, so the rate does not change.

questions.explanation: When the concentration of substrate molecules exceeds that of enzymes, the enzymes become saturated, meaning they are all occupied and working at their maximum capacity. Other options are incorrect because the reaction rate does not increase, decrease, or vary under these conditions.

questions.text: Bond sensitive to change in temperature and pH are respectively:

questions.text: Which cells absorb carbon dioxide in the leaf?

questions.text: What does ATP provide during photosynthesis?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: ATP is primarily a carrier of chemical energy. While it can be utilized to power mechanical processes indirectly through molecular motors, its main function is to provide energy for biochemical reactions.

Correct. ATP functions as a key energy carrier in cells, providing chemical energy stored in its phosphate bonds to drive various cellular processes, including those in photosynthesis.
This option is incorrect because ATP specifically provides chemical energy, which can be converted into other forms of work. However, it is not a direct source of mechanical or physical energy.

questions.text: Cooperation of the two photosystems of the chloroplast is required for_

questions.text: What is the most common electron transport chain?

options.explanation: This is the primary and most common electron transport chain in photosynthesis. Electrons flow linearly through photosystems I and II, leading to the reduction of NADP+ to NADPH. In this process, water is split, releasing oxygen as a byproduct.

This pathway also generates ATP, providing the energy and reducing power needed for the Calvin cycle.”

This conversion involves the removal of one carbon dioxide molecule from pyruvic acid and the transfer of two carbons to coenzyme A (CoA), resulting in the formation of acetyl-CoA.

questions.text: Out of the 6 molecules of G3P, how many molecules are used to make glucose?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This is correct. The Calvin Cycle, the light-independent reactions of photosynthesis, produces three molecules of glyceraldehyde-3-phosphate (G3P) for every three molecules of carbon dioxide that enter the cycle. However, it takes two turns of the Calvin Cycle to produce one molecule of glucose. Each turn produces one molecule of G3P, and three G3P molecules are needed to form one molecule of glucose. Therefore, out of the 6 molecules of G3P produced in two turns of the Calvin Cycle, only 1 molecule is used to make glucose, and the remaining 5 molecules are used to regenerate the molecules needed to continue the cycle.

B) 2 molecules:Option (b) "2 molecules" is not the correct answer either. Two molecules of G3P are also not sufficient to form glucose, as glucose has six carbon atoms and requires more than two G3P molecules.

C) 3 molecules:Option (c) "3 molecules" is an incorrect answer. Three molecules of G3P are used to synthesize glucose. These three G3P molecules combine through a series of chemical reactions to form glucose, which is a six-carbon sugar.

D) 6 molecules:Option (d) "6 molecules" is not the correct answer. While six molecules of G3P are produced during the Calvin cycle, not all of them are used to make glucose. Only three of the G3P molecules are utilized for glucose synthesis.

questions.text: The difference in photosynthesis spectrum and action spectrum occurs due to _.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Carbon dioxide is a reactant in photosynthesis and is not related to the difference in spectra.
Oxygen is a product of photosynthesis and does not explain the difference in spectra.

The difference between the two spectra occurs due to the presence of pigments called carotenoids in the plant cells. Carotenoids are accessory pigments that help capture light energy from wavelengths that chlorophyll, the primary pigment responsible for photosynthesis, cannot effectively absorb. They extend the range of wavelengths that can be utilized by the plant, leading to a difference between the photosynthesis spectrum (absorption spectrum of chlorophyll alone) and the action spectrum (effectiveness of various wavelengths in driving photosynthesis, considering all pigments, including carotenoids).
The absorption spectrum describes the wavelengths that are absorbed or the light that is harvested, while the action spectrum describes the wavelengths that drive photosynthesis.

questions.explanation: Photosynthesis is a complex process that converts light energy into chemical energy, producing glucose and oxygen. The difference between the photosynthesis spectrum and the action spectrum is due to the presence of carotenoids, which extend the range of light wavelengths that plants can utilize for photosynthesis. Additionally, carbon dioxide is a necessary raw material, and oxygen is a byproduct of this vital biological process. The wavelength of light affects the efficiency of photosynthesis, and only certain wavelengths are effectively used by the plant's pigments to drive the process.

questions.text: The Electronic Probability distribution is affected by:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Explanation: The nucleus of an atom, which contains protons and neutrons, plays a crucial role in determining the electronic probability distribution. The positive charge of the nucleus attracts the negatively charged electrons. The closer an electron is to the nucleus, the stronger the attractive force. This influence is described by the concept of effective nuclear charge, which takes into account both the actual nuclear charge and the shielding effect of inner electrons. The electronic probability distribution is affected by the strength of the attraction between the electrons and the nucleus.

Explanation: The atomic number of an element corresponds to the number of protons in its nucleus. The number of protons determines the positive charge in the nucleus. As the atomic number increases, the positive charge in the nucleus also increases, leading to a stronger attraction for electrons. This affects the electronic probability distribution by influencing the arrangement and energy levels of electrons within the atom.

Explanation: The number of atoms in a system does not directly influence the electronic probability distribution of individual atoms. Electronic probability distribution is a property related to the arrangement of electrons within an atom. In a multi-atom system (molecule or solid), the interactions between atoms can influence electron distribution, but the number of atoms alone is not a direct factor in the distribution of electrons around an individual atom.

Explanation: This option is not correct. The electronic probability distribution is indeed affected by both the nucleus of the atom and the atomic number, as explained above. The number of atoms, however, is not a direct factor influencing the electronic probability distribution of an individual atom.

questions.explanation: From the explanation below (PTB I, p. 141), the most suitable choice is "atomic number" because higher atomic number means a greater distance of an electron from the nucleus, and therefore lower electron probability distribution.

questions.text: Planck's constant was named after the scientist?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Einstein: Albert Einstein was a renowned physicist, but he is not directly associated with Planck's constant. However, Einstein made significant contributions to the field of theoretical physics, and he is well-known for his work on the photoelectric effect, which played a role in the development of quantum theory.

Maxwell: James Clerk Maxwell was a Scottish physicist who formulated the classical theory of electromagnetic fields. While Maxwell made crucial contributions to the understanding of electromagnetism, he is not directly linked to Planck's constant. His work laid the foundation for later developments in physics, including the theory of relativity and quantum mechanics.

Newton: Sir Isaac Newton was a mathematician and physicist who made foundational contributions to classical mechanics and the laws of motion. However, Newton's work predates the development of quantum mechanics, and he is not associated with Planck's constant.

Planck: Max Planck was a German physicist who is considered the father of quantum theory. In 1900, he introduced the concept of quantization of energy to explain the observed behavior of blackbody radiation. As part of his groundbreaking work, he introduced a fundamental constant that came to be known as Planck's constant (h). This constant is a crucial parameter in quantum mechanics and is named in honor of Max Planck for his pioneering contributions to the field.

questions.explanation: The Planck constant (Planck's constant) tells us what would be the change in the energy of a photon when the frequency of its electromagnetic wave increases by 1 (In SI Units). It is named after the physicist Max Planck.In SI Units the Planck constant is exactly 6.626×10-34 J·s.

options.explanation: The minimum energy required for an X-ray emission corresponds to the energy difference between the n=2 and n=1 levels in a hydrogen-like ion. For helium (Z=2), this energy level difference matches the energy of the given X-ray wavelength, calculated using the Rydberg formula and Planck's equation.
For Z=3, the energy difference between n=2 and n=1 is larger than the energy of the given X-ray wavelength, which means this transition results in the emission of a higher energy X-ray than provided.
In the case of Z=4, the energy difference between levels n=2 and n=1 is even greater compared to Z=3, thus resulting in even higher energy X-rays than those specified in the question.
With Z=5, the energy transition between n=2 and n=1 levels yields even higher energy X-rays than those mentioned, making it less relevant to the provided wavelength.

questions.explanation: The energy of a photon emitted during a transition between two energy levels in a Bohr atom is given by the equation: △E = λhc = 6.63×10-34 J·s × 3.00×108 m/s / 4.0×10-8 m = 4.97×10-15 J. For a hydrogen-like ion, the energy difference between levels n=2 and n=1 is △E = 13.6 eV × Z2 × (1/n12 - 1/n22), where n1 = 1 and n2 = 2. Calculating for Z=2 gives the energy close to the X-ray energy calculated, thus confirming helium (Z=2) as correct. For higher Z values, the energy difference exceeds the given X-ray energy, eliminating options B, C, and D.

questions.text: The greater the value of the principal quantum number 'n', the distance of an electron from the nucleus will be _.

options.explanation: The distance of an electron from the nucleus does not get lower as the principal quantum number increases. In fact, it gets higher.

questions.text: Positive rays are produced when _ strike the molecules of a gas in a discharge tube.

options.explanation: Low-speed neutrons: Neutrons are neutral particles, so they do not have any charge. They also have a very small mass, so they do not have enough energy to knock out electrons from gas molecules. Therefore, low-speed neutrons cannot produce positive rays.

High-speed neutrons: High-speed neutrons are also neutral particles, but they have a lot of energy. However, they are not affected by electric or magnetic fields, so they cannot be used to produce positive rays.
Low-speed electrons: Low-speed electrons do not have enough energy to knock out electrons from gas molecules. Therefore, low-speed electrons cannot produce positive rays.

Positive rays are produced when high-speed electrons strike the molecules of a gas in a discharge tube. When a high-speed electron strikes a molecule of gas, it can knock out one or more electrons from the molecule. The molecule that has lost electrons becomes a positively charged ion. These positively charged ions are called positive rays. The positive rays are attracted to the negative electrode in the discharge tube, and they can be deflected by a magnetic field. This deflection can be used to measure the charge-to-mass ratio of the positive rays.

questions.explanation: In the Discharge Tube Experiment, high-speed electrons transition from the metal of the cathode and go towards the anode. It may collide with gas molecules present in the tube. Out of many possibilities, one being the gas molecule may lose an electron (pushed out) upon collision, forming a stream of positively charged particles, also known as anode rays. Hence, the particles responsible for the production of positive rays are "high-speed electrons".

The 'p' orbitals (px, py, and pz) are a set of three degenerate orbitals that belong to the second energy level (n=2) in an atom. These orbitals have different orientations in space, and they are a part of the electron cloud surrounding the nucleus. Here's how the 'p' orbitals differ from each other:1. **Orientation in Space:** - px orbital: It is aligned along the x-axis in a Cartesian coordinate system, which means it extends in the horizontal plane. - py orbital: It is aligned along the y-axis in a Cartesian coordinate system, which means it extends in the vertical plane. - pz orbital: It is aligned along the z-axis in a Cartesian coordinate system, which means it extends in the direction perpendicular to both the x and y axes (i.e., it points along the vertical axis).2. **Shape:** - All three 'p' orbitals have a dumbbell shape with a node (region of zero probability of finding an electron) at the nucleus and two lobes of opposite phase (one positive and one negative) on either side of the node. These lobes represent the regions of high probability of finding the electron.3. **Degeneracy:** - The 'p' orbitals have the same energy, making them degenerate orbitals. This means that they have identical energy levels in a single atom.4. **Quantum Numbers:** - Each 'p' orbital is defined by three quantum numbers: n (principal quantum number), l (azimuthal quantum number), and ml (magnetic quantum number). - For the 'p' orbitals: n=2, l=1, and ml can take values of -1, 0, and +1, corresponding to the px, py, and pz orbitals, respectively.It's important to note that 'p' orbitals can hold up to two electrons each (a total of six electrons for all three 'p' orbitals in an atom). These orbitals play a crucial role in the formation of chemical bonds and contribute significantly to the molecular geometry and bonding properties of molecules.

Hence, this option is not correct.

Furthermore, there are three p orbitals; they are the Px, Py, and Pz orbitals.

questions.text: If a molecule contains 4 electron pairs, the shape of molecule will be:
